label: Game theory & decision theory

node.label: Game theory & decision theory
node.definition: Utilities, rational choice, strategic interaction, and sequential decisions formalize action under uncertainty.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Game theory & decision theory is modeled as depending on Probability theory, Optimization theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Labels, targets, rewards & preferences, Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states, Reinforcement learning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Labels, targets, rewards & preferences
atlas downstream link to Markov chains, MDPs, POMDPs & belief states
atlas downstream link to Reinforcement learning
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Optimization theory.

dependencies.rationale: Game theory & decision theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
Game theory & decision theory rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Optimization theory.
